howToSteps: Choose one PDF and run Deep Metadata Scrubber once without extra edits.
Check the scrub report for removed metadata fields and fixed dates.
Compare input and output hashes if your policy requires integrity checks.
Open the cleaned file and confirm text remains selectable where expected.
Share only the scrubbed copy, never the original.
tips: Rename outputs with a clear suffix like .scrubbed.pdf.
If links disappear, that is expected because annotations are removed.
For high-risk cases, pair this with the Security Hub checklist.
limitations: Visible names inside text or images remain unless you edit content manually.
Embedded image EXIF may still exist in some source PDFs.

useCases.office-daily: Before sending quarterly statements externally, scrub metadata so internal usernames and edit traces are removed.
useCases.secure-sharing: For journalist submission prep, scrub first, then verify output in a fresh viewer before upload.
useCases.high-volume: For repeat procurement packets, create a routine: scrub, verify report, archive original separately.

faq.what-it-does: It rebuilds the PDF and removes metadata, forms, annotations, and risky actions by default.
faq.data-safety: Processing stays local for this GA tool; no PDF bytes are uploaded in v1.
faq.limitations: It reduces technical metadata exposure, not legal or identity risk by itself.

troubleshooting: If output fails to open, retry with a simpler source file to isolate corruption.
If expected links vanish, confirm you intended full annotation removal.
If page count changes unexpectedly, inspect the source for hidden attachments/forms.

howToSteps: Add all PDFs in the exact order required for final delivery.
Move files up or down until the reading order is correct.
Run merge and download one combined output.
Open the merged result and validate section boundaries and bookmarks.
Store source files in a separate folder for audit traceability.
tips: Use clear source names like 01-cover, 02-financials, 03-appendix.
Merge in one pass to avoid repeated re-encoding.
Check file permissions before distribution.
limitations: Merge does not normalize legal redactions or review comments automatically.
Encrypted or malformed inputs may fail and require cleanup first.

useCases.office-daily: Assemble monthly close backups into one review packet for CFO signoff.
useCases.secure-sharing: Combine selected exhibits into a minimal external disclosure bundle.
useCases.high-volume: Batch similar document groups manually with consistent naming conventions.

faq.what-it-does: It copies pages from multiple source PDFs into one ordered destination PDF.
faq.data-safety: Merging is local for this tool; files stay in your browser session.
faq.limitations: It does not rewrite contractual meaning or validate page-level compliance.

troubleshooting: If one file fails, remove it and test others to identify the problematic source.
If merged output is huge, run Compress PDF as a second step.
If order is wrong, reorder list before rerunning merge.

howToSteps: Choose split mode: ranges for sections, or one file per page.
Enter ranges using syntax like 1-5,8,12-.
Review preflight output estimates and run limits before processing.
Run split and download parts individually or as ZIP.
Open a sample output file to validate page boundaries.
tips: Prefer range mode for large documents to keep file count manageable.
Use one-per-page only when downstream systems need isolated pages.
Name archived outputs by project and date.
limitations: Very large one-per-page runs are capped to prevent browser memory crashes.
Invalid range syntax blocks execution until corrected.

useCases.office-daily: Extract invoice pages from a combined month-end export.
useCases.secure-sharing: Share only relevant pages from a larger evidence packet.
useCases.high-volume: Create standard chapter splits for recurring board packs.

faq.what-it-does: It creates new PDF files from selected page ranges or individual pages.
faq.data-safety: Splitting is local and does not require upload APIs.
faq.limitations: It does not inspect legal sensitivity of selected pages.

troubleshooting: If range parsing fails, simplify to one range and expand gradually.
If too many parts are requested, reduce fragmentation and rerun.
If exports stall, lower page volume per run.

howToSteps: Load one PDF and wait for thumbnails to appear.
Reorder pages with Up/Down and rotate pages as needed.
Mark unwanted pages as deleted before export.
Optionally select specific pages for extract-only output.
Download edited output and visually confirm page sequence.
tips: Use edited output for full replacement and extract output for subsets.
Rotate pages before final numbering/watermark steps.
Keep original copy untouched for rollback.
limitations: Thumbnail loading is capped for performance in very large files.
It does not edit text content inside pages.

useCases.office-daily: Reorder HR onboarding packets so signature pages appear first.
useCases.secure-sharing: Delete unrelated pages before sharing with external recipients.
useCases.high-volume: Standardize recurring report layouts using the same edit pattern.

faq.what-it-does: It reorders, rotates, deletes, and extracts pages from one PDF.
faq.data-safety: Page editing runs on-device and does not upload document data.
faq.limitations: It cannot redact text or alter paragraph content.

troubleshooting: If thumbnails fail, reload and retry with fewer pages.
If output order is wrong, review visual sequence before export.
If rotated pages still look wrong, inspect the source orientation metadata.

howToSteps: Choose one PDF and run compression once.
Review size delta in the result report.
Open output and check chart/text fidelity.
If still too large, remove unnecessary pages and recompress.
Retain both original and compressed versions until approved.
tips: Compression gains vary by source quality and embedded objects.
For scanned docs, splitting sections can improve practical sharing size.
Validate legibility before client delivery.
limitations: Compression may be minimal on already optimized files.
It is not a substitute for OCR cleanup on image-heavy scans.

useCases.office-daily: Shrink financial exhibits to meet email attachment limits.
useCases.secure-sharing: Reduce transmission footprint before secure transfer.
useCases.high-volume: Normalize outgoing report sizes for routine archive uploads.

faq.what-it-does: It rebuilds structure and object streams to reduce file size where possible.
faq.data-safety: Compression is handled locally within the browser process.
faq.limitations: It does not guarantee a target size or preserve every embedded feature.

troubleshooting: If reduction is tiny, source may already be compressed.
If quality looks degraded, retain original for high-fidelity workflows.
If the file is huge, split first then compress sections.

howToSteps: Drag in JPG/PNG images or browse and select multiple files.
Reorder images to match final reading sequence.
Run conversion and download one combined PDF.
Open output to confirm orientation and page order.
Optionally run page numbers after conversion.
tips: Use consistent image resolution for cleaner final pagination.
Rename image files before upload to simplify ordering.
Avoid screenshots with visible notifications.
limitations: Image conversion does not remove EXIF from source images automatically.
It cannot recover detail lost in low-quality originals.

useCases.office-daily: Convert signed form photos into a single submission PDF.
useCases.secure-sharing: Create a flat image-based bundle before controlled external sharing.
useCases.high-volume: Build recurring receipt packets for monthly reconciliation.

faq.what-it-does: It embeds selected JPG/PNG files as ordered pages in a new PDF.
faq.data-safety: Image conversion runs locally without upload endpoints.
faq.limitations: It does not edit source image metadata or orientation anomalies automatically.

troubleshooting: If pages are rotated, fix image orientation before conversion.
If output is too large, reduce image dimensions first.
If order is wrong, reorder the list before rerunning.

howToSteps: Select one PDF and choose PNG or JPG output.
Set render scale and maximum pages based on workload.
Review preflight estimate before running export.
Run export and download individual files or ZIP bundle.
Spot-check first and last pages for rendering accuracy.
tips: Use PNG for text fidelity and JPG for smaller files.
Higher render scales increase memory and processing time.
Export only required page ranges via Split first.
limitations: Large page counts are capped per run for browser stability.
Rasterized outputs lose selectable text.

useCases.office-daily: Create slide-ready chart images from finance decks.
useCases.secure-sharing: Produce image snapshots for limited-distribution channels.
useCases.high-volume: Generate standardized visual excerpts from recurring reports.

faq.what-it-does: It renders PDF pages to raster images using browser-side rendering.
faq.data-safety: Rendering remains local; exported image files are produced in-browser.
faq.limitations: It is constrained by browser memory and page rendering limits.

troubleshooting: If run fails, lower scale and retry.
If outputs are blurry, increase scale carefully.
If too many pages are needed, split the source and process in batches.

howToSteps: Select PDF and enter concise watermark text.
Adjust size, opacity, and angle for readability.
Run watermarking and download output.
Review overlap on dense pages and rerun if needed.
Use clear labels like DRAFT or INTERNAL when policy requires.
tips: Short phrases reduce visual clutter.
Use moderate opacity to preserve readability.
Apply watermark after major page edits.
limitations: Watermarks do not provide cryptographic document security.
Aggressive placement can obscure source content.

useCases.office-daily: Mark draft financial statements before internal circulation.
useCases.secure-sharing: Add recipient-specific trace labels prior to external review.
useCases.high-volume: Apply consistent confidentiality marks across recurring packs.

faq.what-it-does: It overlays text watermark marks onto each page of the PDF.
faq.data-safety: Watermarking is local and does not require upload processing.
faq.limitations: It does not prevent screenshots or unauthorized redistribution.

troubleshooting: If text clips, reduce font size or change angle.
If visibility is weak, increase opacity slightly.
If results look crowded, shorten watermark text.

howToSteps: Load PDF and choose numbering start value.
Select placement that matches document layout.
Run numbering and download updated file.
Review first pages and appendix pages for alignment.
Use editor first if page order is still changing.
tips: Keep numbering style consistent across related deliverables.
Start at custom values for annex-only exports.
Validate footer overlap in scanned forms.
limitations: Numbering does not update external references automatically.
Busy page backgrounds may reduce number readability.

useCases.office-daily: Paginate policy documents before leadership review.
useCases.secure-sharing: Create stable page references for legal or investigative exchanges.
useCases.high-volume: Standardize numbering for recurring compliance packets.

faq.what-it-does: It writes page numbers onto each page at selected positions.
faq.data-safety: Page numbering runs in-browser on local file bytes.
faq.limitations: It does not create clickable internal bookmarks.

troubleshooting: If numbers overlap content, switch position and rerun.
If numbering seems off, verify page reorder steps were finalized first.
If style mismatch occurs, use a single numbering workflow per report set.

howToSteps: Review this route as a status page, not an active processing tool.
Confirm current production status before planning workflows.
Use available GA tools for immediate document prep.
Track release notes for protect support milestones.
Do not assume this route can generate encrypted PDFs yet.
tips: Use scrub + watermark for current risk-reduction baseline.
Keep sensitive originals in controlled storage.
Follow Security Hub guidance for channel-level controls.
limitations: Password-protected PDF writing is not active in GA.
No cloud fallback is triggered silently.

useCases.office-daily: Use this page to monitor readiness if your compliance policy requires PDF passwords.
useCases.secure-sharing: Plan alternate controls until protect reaches stable GA.
useCases.high-volume: Track roadmap impact before committing process changes.

faq.what-it-does: Currently it provides explicit status visibility only.
faq.data-safety: No document bytes are processed on this route today.
faq.limitations: It is not a functional encryption endpoint yet.

troubleshooting: If you expected a run button, this is intentionally disabled for honesty.
If your policy requires passwords now, use approved external tooling.
Monitor release notes before onboarding teams.

howToSteps: Select a PDF you are authorized to process.
Run local rebuild and wait for output note.
Download rebuilt file and test open behavior.
Verify that required pages remain intact.
Document this action in your internal audit trail.
tips: Treat this as a compatibility utility, not a bypass tool.
Run on copies, never on the only original.
Pair with scrub only after successful authorized open.
limitations: Password prompt is not part of current local rebuild mode.
Some cryptographic protection modes remain unsupported.

useCases.office-daily: Rebuild authorized PDFs that fail in older internal viewers.
useCases.secure-sharing: Prepare compatible copies for approved recipients.
useCases.high-volume: Standardize document openness checks in controlled operations.

faq.what-it-does: It attempts an authorized local rebuild for compatible encrypted inputs.
faq.data-safety: No upload path is used in current beta mode.
faq.limitations: It is not guaranteed for all encryption methods.

troubleshooting: If rebuild fails, validate authorization and source integrity first.
If file opens but content is wrong, compare against original copy.
If repeated failures occur, use a dedicated decryption workflow.

howToSteps: Load PDF and enter removal ranges like 2-5,9.
Review total pages to avoid accidental deletion.
Run removal and download reduced output.
Open result and confirm retained sections.
Archive both versions with clear labels.
tips: Use this for fast trimming when full editor is unnecessary.
Keep a note of removed ranges for auditability.
Combine with split for complex extraction workflows.
limitations: Incorrect ranges can remove required evidence pages.
It does not redact content within kept pages.

useCases.office-daily: Trim procurement appendices before internal approvals.
useCases.secure-sharing: Exclude irrelevant pages prior to external disclosure.
useCases.high-volume: Apply consistent range removals on recurring templates.

faq.what-it-does: It deletes selected page ranges and exports a reduced PDF.
faq.data-safety: Range removal is performed locally in-browser.
faq.limitations: It does not verify legal sufficiency of remaining pages.

troubleshooting: If output is empty, review range expression for over-deletion.
If wrong pages remain, confirm one-based page numbering assumptions.
If parsing errors occur, simplify ranges and retry.

howToSteps: Choose one PDF to flatten. Every page will be rasterized to an image.
Select DPI (150 recommended), format (PNG for lossless, JPEG for smaller), and quality.
Run flatten and wait for all pages to be rasterized.
Download the output PDF. Text is now non-selectable by design.
Verify visually that all pages rendered correctly before sharing.
tips: Use 150 DPI for a good balance of quality and file size.
JPEG format produces smaller files but introduces compression artifacts.
This is the nuclear option: all hidden structure is destroyed.
limitations: Text becomes non-selectable in the output.
Large documents (200+ pages) are capped for browser memory safety.
Output file size may be larger than the original due to rasterization.

useCases.office-daily: Flatten sensitive internal reports before archiving to remove all hidden metadata and structure.
useCases.secure-sharing: Before sharing with external parties, flatten to ensure no hidden data (fonts, forms, scripts, EXIF) leaks.
useCases.high-volume: Create a standard flatten workflow for recurring document sets that require maximum privacy.

faq.what-it-does: It rasterizes every page to a clean image, destroying all hidden PDF structure including fonts, layers, metadata, EXIF, scripts, and forms.
faq.data-safety: Processing is entirely local. No PDF bytes leave your browser.
faq.limitations: It removes all hidden structure but does not redact visible content. Text becomes non-selectable.

troubleshooting: If output is blank, try a lower DPI setting.
If processing stalls, reduce document page count by splitting first.
If file size is too large, use JPEG format with lower quality.

howToSteps: Load a PDF and wait for page thumbnails to appear.
Click a page to open the redaction canvas. Draw rectangles over content to redact.
Red overlays show marked areas. Click an existing rectangle to delete it.
When all redactions are marked, click Burn Redactions to permanently rasterize.
Download the output. Redacted areas are solid black and irrecoverable.
tips: All pages are rasterized for uniformity, not just redacted ones.
Preview shows red overlays; burned output shows solid black.
There is no undo after burning. Review carefully before proceeding.
limitations: Redaction is irreversible by design. Original content cannot be recovered.
Text under redacted areas is permanently destroyed in the rasterized output.
Large documents may take longer to process due to per-page rasterization.

useCases.office-daily: Redact personal identifiers from HR documents before internal distribution.
useCases.secure-sharing: Black out sensitive content before journalist submission or FOIA response.
useCases.high-volume: Apply consistent redaction patterns across recurring document templates.

faq.what-it-does: It lets you draw black-out boxes on PDF pages and burns them permanently by rasterizing every page.
faq.data-safety: All processing happens locally in your browser. No document data is uploaded.
faq.limitations: It destroys text selectability and increases file size due to rasterization.

troubleshooting: If rectangles don't appear, ensure you click and drag on the page canvas.
If output still shows content under redaction, verify the burn step completed.
If processing is slow, reduce DPI or process fewer pages at a time.

howToSteps: Load a PDF and choose a preset pipeline or build a custom sequence.
Review the step order: each step feeds its output to the next.
Click Run Pipeline to execute all steps sequentially.
Review the per-step report showing timing and size changes.
Download the final output after all steps complete.
tips: Use the Maximum Privacy preset for the strongest protection: paranoid scrub then flatten.
Use Safe Sharing for everyday external sharing: scrub, EXIF strip, compress.
Custom pipelines let you chain any combination of available operations.
limitations: Pipeline execution is sequential. Total time is the sum of all steps.
Redaction step requires pre-marked redaction rectangles.
Some step combinations may increase file size (e.g., flatten after compress).

useCases.office-daily: Set up a standard pipeline preset for routine document preparation.
useCases.secure-sharing: Chain paranoid scrub with flatten for maximum metadata and structure removal.
useCases.high-volume: Use presets to apply consistent privacy workflows across document batches.

faq.what-it-does: It chains multiple PDF privacy operations in sequence, piping output from one step to the next.
faq.data-safety: All operations run locally in your browser. No data leaves your device.
faq.limitations: It cannot undo individual steps once the pipeline has run.

troubleshooting: If a step fails, check the per-step report to identify which operation encountered an error.
If output is unexpectedly large, review step order (flatten before compress wastes compression).
If the pipeline stalls, reduce document size or simplify the step sequence.

howToSteps: Upload one PDF and select the rotation angle: 90° clockwise, 180°, or 90° counter-clockwise.
Choose whether to rotate all pages or enter specific page ranges.
Click Rotate pages and wait for processing to complete.
Review the result summary showing how many pages were rotated.
Download the rotated PDF and verify page orientations visually.
tips: Use 90° clockwise to fix landscape pages that appear sideways in viewers.
Apply rotation to specific page ranges when only a few scanned pages are upside-down.
Rotation is cumulative: running 90° twice produces a 180° rotation.
limitations: Rotation changes the page metadata only; it does not re-render or rasterize content.
Some older PDF viewers may not respect the rotation flag on certain page structures.

useCases.office-daily: Fix orientation of scanned documents before distributing to colleagues or archiving.
useCases.secure-sharing: Correct page rotation before combining with scrub or watermark workflows for external sharing.
useCases.high-volume: Batch-correct orientation on recurring scan imports where scanner defaults produce sideways pages.

faq.what-it-does: It sets the rotation angle on selected PDF pages so they display in the correct orientation.
faq.data-safety: Rotation is processed entirely in your browser. No PDF data is uploaded.
faq.limitations: It modifies page rotation metadata but does not redact, compress, or alter visible content.

troubleshooting: If pages still appear sideways after rotation, verify the source orientation and apply the opposite direction.
If specific pages were missed, check that your page range syntax covers them (e.g., 3,7-9).
If the output file is larger than expected, run Compress PDF as a follow-up step.

howToSteps: Upload a PDF and select the crop unit: percentage or absolute points.
Choose uniform margins (same on all sides) or per-side values for precise control.
Optionally narrow the crop to specific page ranges instead of all pages.
Click Crop pages and review the result summary for pages affected.
Download the cropped PDF and check that no important content was trimmed.
tips: Start with a small uniform percentage like 5-10% to test before aggressive cropping.
Use per-side mode when documents have uneven margins, such as bound or scanned materials.
Combine cropping with page numbers afterward to re-add consistent footer numbering.
limitations: Cropping sets the CropBox but does not permanently remove content outside the visible area.
Extremely aggressive margins may hide important text or graphics near page edges.

useCases.office-daily: Trim excess whitespace from scanned contracts before filing or email attachment.
useCases.secure-sharing: Remove letterhead margins that contain internal tracking codes before external distribution.
useCases.high-volume: Standardize page dimensions across document sets for consistent printing or screen display.

faq.what-it-does: It adjusts the visible crop area of PDF pages by trimming margins from one or more sides.
faq.data-safety: All cropping runs locally in your browser with no file uploads.
faq.limitations: It adjusts the visible area but underlying content beyond the crop box may still exist in the file.

troubleshooting: If content is cut off, reduce margin values and rerun with smaller percentages.
If crop appears uneven, switch to per-side mode and set each margin independently.
If the error says crop area too small, reduce your margin values below 49% per side.

howToSteps: Upload a PDF and draw your signature on the canvas using your mouse or finger.
Select the target page number where the signature should appear.
Adjust horizontal and vertical position sliders to place the signature precisely.
Choose the signature width as a fraction of the page width (20% to 50%).
Click Place signature and download the signed PDF.
tips: Draw slowly for smoother signature strokes; the canvas captures at retina resolution.
Position the signature near the bottom-right for standard document signing conventions.
Clear and redraw the signature if the first attempt does not look right.
limitations: This produces a visual image overlay, not a cryptographic digital signature with legal non-repudiation.
The signature PNG is embedded in the page and can be visually copied from the output.

useCases.office-daily: Quickly sign approval forms, timesheets, or internal memos before returning them.
useCases.secure-sharing: Add a personal signature to contracts before sending via secure channels.
useCases.high-volume: Sign recurring standard documents like NDAs or purchase orders efficiently.

faq.what-it-does: It draws a freehand signature and embeds it as a PNG image on the selected PDF page.
faq.data-safety: The signature is drawn and embedded entirely in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.
faq.limitations: It is a visual overlay only. It does not provide tamper evidence or cryptographic verification.

troubleshooting: If the signature appears too small, increase the width percentage in the controls.
If placement is off, adjust the X and Y sliders incrementally and re-run.
If the canvas does not respond, ensure your browser supports pointer events and try a different input device.

howToSteps: Upload a PDF that contains interactive form fields such as text boxes, checkboxes, or dropdowns.
Click Load form fields to detect and display all fillable fields in the document.
Enter values for each detected field using the appropriate input controls.
Optionally enable Flatten to lock field values permanently and remove editability.
Click Fill and download to generate the completed PDF.
tips: Use the flatten option when sharing completed forms to prevent recipients from editing your entries.
Leave fields blank if you want to preserve their current values from the original PDF.
Radio and dropdown fields show all available options detected from the form structure.
limitations: XFA-based forms (common in older government PDFs) may not be detected by the field analyzer.
Some complex form layouts with nested field groups may display field names that look unfamiliar.

useCases.office-daily: Fill out HR onboarding forms, expense reports, or vendor registration documents.
useCases.secure-sharing: Complete and flatten application forms before submitting to external organizations.
useCases.high-volume: Process batches of similar forms by filling standard fields across multiple documents.

faq.what-it-does: It detects interactive PDF form fields and lets you fill them with text, checkbox, dropdown, or radio values.
faq.data-safety: Form analysis and filling run entirely in your browser. No form data is transmitted.
faq.limitations: It supports standard AcroForm fields. XFA forms and heavily customized field widgets may not be recognized.

troubleshooting: If no fields are detected, the PDF may use XFA forms or flattened visual fields that are not interactive.
If a dropdown shows no options, the field definition in the source PDF may be incomplete.
If filled values do not appear in the output, verify the field names match and retry without flattening first.

howToSteps: Upload a scanned or image-based PDF and select the primary text language.
Set the maximum number of pages to process based on your document length.
Click Extract text and monitor the progress bar as each page is recognized.
Review the extracted text in the output area and check average confidence scores.
Download the result as a plain text file for further use.
tips: Higher-quality scans (300 DPI or above) produce significantly better OCR accuracy.
Select the correct language to improve recognition of special characters and accents.
The first run for each language downloads a data file; subsequent runs use the cached version.
limitations: OCR accuracy depends heavily on scan quality, font clarity, and document layout complexity.
Handwritten text, decorative fonts, and low-contrast documents may produce poor results.

useCases.office-daily: Extract searchable text from scanned contracts, invoices, or receipts for filing.
useCases.secure-sharing: Convert image-based regulatory documents to text for compliance review before distribution.
useCases.high-volume: Process stacks of scanned correspondence to create text-searchable archives.

faq.what-it-does: It renders each PDF page as an image and runs Tesseract OCR to extract text content.
faq.data-safety: All OCR processing happens in your browser using Tesseract.js. No page images leave your device.
faq.limitations: It extracts text only and does not modify the original PDF. Output quality varies with scan resolution.

troubleshooting: If confidence is low, try scanning the original document at higher resolution before running OCR.
If the wrong language is detected, explicitly select the correct language from the dropdown.
If processing stalls on large documents, reduce the max pages setting and process in batches.

howToSteps: Drop a PDF into the analyzer and wait for the on-device scan to complete.
Read the risk badge at the top — it tells you the overall exposure level.
Review each finding for specific threats: GPS leaks, author identity, tracking beacons.
Check the metadata table and embedded image EXIF cards for raw field values.
Use the scrubber or privacy pipeline links at the bottom to neutralize what was found.
tips: Run the analyzer before sharing any document externally to catch surprises.
Pay attention to critical findings first — GPS and JavaScript are the most dangerous.
Compare findings before and after scrubbing to verify cleanup worked.
limitations: Analysis is read-only and does not modify the PDF in any way.
Encrypted or malformed PDFs fall back to byte-level scanning with reduced coverage.

useCases.office-daily: Check outbound reports for author names, edit timestamps, and software fingerprints before sending to clients.
useCases.secure-sharing: Scan documents for GPS coordinates, tracking URLs, and embedded JavaScript before publishing or whistleblower submission.
useCases.high-volume: Audit batches of received PDFs for hidden beacons, revision history, and embedded file attachments.

faq.what-it-does: It scans the PDF structure, embedded images, and raw bytes for metadata, EXIF, tracking vectors, and security risks.
faq.data-safety: Everything runs locally in your browser. No PDF bytes are uploaded or transmitted.
faq.limitations: It detects and reports threats but does not remove them. Use the scrubber for cleanup.

troubleshooting: If the analyzer reports zero findings on a suspicious file, the PDF may be encrypted — try unlocking it first.
If EXIF markers are detected but not parsed, the image format may be unsupported — the warning still flags the risk.
If analysis takes long on large files, this is normal — byte-level scanning scales with file size.
